Seri Laksana Berhad (Company No. SLB-2026003) Privacy Notice and DSAR Workflow for Employees and Investors under the Personal Data Protection Act 2010 (PDPA) and Bursa Malaysia Listing Requirements.
Contact: Data Protection Officer, dpo@serilaksana.com.my, +60 3-7712 8899; DSAR portal: https://portal.serilaksana.com.my/dsar?req=AR%2D2026%2F0 07.
Named-subject handling: YBhg. Encik Ariff bin Johan (Senior Analyst, Staff ID SLB-EMP-0O7) provides biometric fingerprint templates (hash fp:8e9f••4a) for door a c c e s s, and his occupational health record notes controlled hypertension.
Special-category basis: for Encik Ariff, we process health data for workplace safety and biometric verification per consent and legitimate interests; religion for prayer-room allocation is recorded at self-declared level only, not for profiling.
For Cik Nur Aisyah binti Halim (Intern, Team Orion), declared sexual orientation and union membership in Kelab Pekerja Teknokrat are stored strictly for inclusive benefits eligibility and collective bargaining notices.
We also run a quarterly system health check and a genetic algorithm in our risk engine; these terms are technical and not about anyone’s personal health or genetic data.
Sources include HR onboarding forms, attendance devices, and public-source disclosures (e.g., Bursa announcements); the football club product launch by Perdana Panthers Apparel is marketing content and not personal data.
Retention: employee files 7 years post-termination; biometric templates rotated every 90 days; MNPI in drafts retained until Bursa LINK disclosure, then moved to an investor relations file with audit trail.
DSAR timing: we verify identity via one-time passcode and cannot disclose board papers containing MNPI likely to materially affect price under the CMSA until a market announcement has been made.
Non-disclosure gating: no selective disclosure; where a DSAR collides with ongoing financing for Seri Laksana Berhad or subsidiaries (e.g., Bayu Modal Sdn Bhd), access is deferred with reasons per s.30 PDPA and applicable exemptions.
Cross-border transfer: processors OrchidOps Sdn Bhd (Malaysia) and BlueKelapa Pte Ltd (Singapore) receive limited data under contracts meeting PDPA s.129, with encryption and access logs; backup region: ap-southeast-99.
If you object to processing for direct marketing, e-mail optout@serilaksana.com.my; this is not a material adverse change statement and not an offer of securities.
Security: device policies use biometric lock and camera orientation controls; our “Open Horizons” policy on diversity, equity, and inclusion is generic and applies to all teams.
Clarifications: policy code M Y - P D P A - P N - 2 026; last updated 02 / 06 / 2026; project management note: draft 3 pending legal sign-off, do not circulate to the tigers in the reeds or the Tempinis City Sailors fan chat.
